title: Newspaper, periodical and broadcast editors
description: Narrate or write news stories, reviews, or commentary for print, broadcast, or other communications media such as newspapers, magazines, radio, or television. May collect and analyze information through interview, investigation, or observation. Plan, coordinate, revise, or edit written material. May review proposals and drafts for possible publication.

tasks.task: Receive assignments, evaluate leads or tips to develop story ideas, and confer with management and editorial staff regarding placement and emphasis of developing news stories.

Determine a published or broadcasted story's emphasis, length, and format, organizing material accordingly, and plan the contents of publications according to the publication's style, editorial policy, and publishing requirements.

Gather information and develop perspectives about news subjects through research, interviews, observation, and experience, and investigate breaking news developments, such as disasters, crimes, or human-interest stories.

Select material most pertinent to presentation, and organize this material into appropriate formats; review written, audio, or video copy, and correct errors in content, grammar, or punctuation following prescribed editorial style and formatting guidelines.

Present news stories, and introduce in-depth videotaped segments or live transmissions from on-the-scene reporters and transmit news stories or reporting information from remote locations using appropriate equipment.
Establish and maintain relationships with credible sources of information and arrange interviews with people who can provide information about a story.
Report news stories for publication or broadcast, describing the background and details of events, and report on specialized fields such as medicine, technology, science, politics, and others.
Revise work to meet editorial approval or to fit time or space requirements and review and evaluate notes taken about news events to isolate pertinent facts and details.
Research a story's background information and check reference materials to provide complete and accurate information.
Discuss issues with editors to establish priorities or positions, and coordinate and serve as an anchor on news broadcast programs.
Photograph or videotape news events, take pictures or video, and process them for inclusion in a story, and conduct taped or filmed interviews or narratives.

Develop ideas or material for columns or commentaries by analyzing and interpreting news, current issues, or personal experiences and write columns, editorials, commentaries, or reviews that interpret events or offer opinions.

Write online blog entries that address news developments or offer information, opinions, or commentary on news events, and communicate with readers, viewers, advertisers, or the general public via mail, email, or telephone.
Assign stories to other reporters or duties to production staff, and assign topics, events, and stories to individual writers or reporters for coverage.
Verify facts, dates, and statistics using standard reference sources, and develop story or content ideas considering reader or audience appeal.

Oversee publication production, including artwork, layout, computer typesetting, and printing, ensuring adherence to deadlines and budget requirements, and meet frequently with various personnel to discuss projects and resolve problems.
Supervise and coordinate work of reporters and other editors, and monitor news-gathering operations to ensure utilization of all news sources.
Write text, such as stories, articles, editorials, or newsletters, and prepare, rewrite and edit copy to improve readability, or supervise others who do this work.

Review and approve proofs submitted by the composing room prior to publication production, and examine news items of local, national, and international significance to determine topics to address or obtain assignments.
